terms.notes: IDENTITY LABEL for systems. NOT grammatical/numerical plurality. Translate as identity marker akin to 'queer' or 'neurodivergent'.
Umbrella term for the experience of multiple consciousnesses in one body. NOT a grammatical or mathematical concept. Community-preferred synonym: 'multiplicity'.
Community synonym for plurality; used interchangeably.
A person who is not plural. Neologism; many languages lack a direct equivalent — retain as English loanword or use a descriptive phrase.

A COLLECTIVE OF HEADMATES sharing one body. Also the primary user account in Pluralscape. Translate using words denoting a group of people, household, team, or collective. NOT computational ('operating system'), biological, or mechanical.
A headmate within a system; an individual consciousness. Preferred over 'alter' or 'personality'.
Community-preferred synonym for 'member' — conveys companionship. Often retained as a loanword or translated laterally (e.g., German 'Systemmitglied').
Synonym for headmate; community-generated portmanteau.
A less-distinct headmate; a fragment with low psychological elaboration. Metaphor: 'a facet of a gem'.
A system with many hundreds or thousands of headmates. Usually retains 'poly-' prefix across target languages.
Polyfragmented system — an exceptionally large, fragmented system. Retain 'poly-' prefix.
Taking EXECUTIVE CONTROL of the body, handling sensation and daily activities. Translate as 'being in control' or 'emerging'. NOT a physical front/facade (avoid Spanish 'frente' for this sense).
The metaphorical driver's seat — where the fronting headmate is. NOT a military line, building facade, or weather front. Community-equivalent: 'in command', 'at the wheel'.
The event of consciousness transfer between headmates. NOT an electrical switch or a swap of physical objects. Translate as psychological transition of control.
The process of switching (see 'switch'). Psychological transition, not mechanical.
Multiple headmates fronting simultaneously, sharing control — tracked in Pluralscape as overlapping time ranges.
Two or more headmates simultaneously aware of the external world (distinct from co-fronting, which adds control).
Thoughts, feelings, or identities of multiple headmates mixing — confused identity. Translate as cognitive/emotional merging.
Fronter identity is indistinct — used as a Pluralscape custom-front option when the fronter is unclear.
A headmate cannot stop fronting or switch away. Involuntary state.
A headmate continuously drawn to front despite switching attempts.
The system's internal mental landscape. Synonyms: headspace, wonderland. Translate as 'inner world' / 'mental space' — low ambiguity risk.
Synonym for innerworld.
Synonym for innerworld, common in older and tulpamancy communities.
A distinct system nested within a single headmate of a larger system. Prefix 'sub-' usually transfers universally.
A semi-independent system sharing the same body — more autonomous than a subsystem.
Systems whose headmates live autonomous lives in an alternate plane when not fronting.
PLURALSCAPE-SPECIFIC: an abstract cognitive state logged like a member (e.g., 'Dissociated', 'Blurry'). Not a headmate.
PLURALSCAPE-SPECIFIC: tag-based access control. Members with matching buckets are visible to a given friend via intersection logic.
A position a headmate takes in the system — a job, attribute, compulsion, or affinity.
The headmate who fronts most often and manages daily external life. NOT a computer server, biological parasite carrier, or party host. Community sense: 'primary occupant' / 'executive manager'.
Shared host role across multiple headmates.
A headmate who shields the system. Subtypes: physical, emotional, sexual. Translate with a protective-guardian sense.
A traumatized headmate who harms the system as a flawed protective mechanism. PSYCHOLOGICAL term, NOT legal 'prosecutor'.
A persecutor who lashes out externally to protect the system (hyper-vigilant).
A headmate controlling access to fronting, memory barriers, and switching. Sense: guardian of consciousness access, NOT physical gate attendant. (Also used pejoratively in discourse — see 'sysmed'.)
A nurturing headmate, often tending littles or body exhaustion.
A headmate who eases panic and de-escalates triggers. NOT a baby pacifier or topical ointment. Translate as active noun ('Calmer', 'Regulator').
A headmate who holds traumatic memories or PTSD responses for the system.
A headmate who embodies severe comorbid symptoms (chronic pain, phobias), sparing others.
A headmate managing internal information and memories.
A headmate with deep structural knowledge of the system. Clinical acronym: ISH.
A headmate managing internal logistics and the innerworld.
Synonym for overseer; tends the innerworld.
A headmate ensuring survival habits — medication, sleep, diet.
A headmate managing money, bills, and impulse control.
A child-aged headmate (NOUN). DO NOT translate as adjective 'small'. Use 'child' / 'youngster' / 'junior' noun form. Drives 'Littles Safe Mode' simplified UI. Synonym: 'syskid'.
A teen-aged headmate (noun form).
An adult headmate, especially in a young body.
A headmate whose internal age shifts based on stress or environment.
How the system/headmate came to be. Types: traumagenic, endogenic, adaptive, created, from-birth, spiritual, mixed-origin.
Plurality formed as a defense against severe childhood trauma. Clinically-aligned origin.
Broader umbrella covering traumagenic plus systems formed to adapt to neurodivergence or chronic stress.
Plurality formed without trauma. Umbrella for non-clinical origins.
Systems formed through intentional creation (e.g., tulpamancy).
Systems that have always been plural.
Plurality from metaphysical origin — multiple souls, possession.
System formed from multiple causes combined.
An autonomous, sentient headmate deliberately created via meditative practice. Retain as loanword.
The host who created a tulpa. Retain as loanword.
The practice of creating tulpas. Retain as loanword.
The meditative cognitive process of creating or strengthening a tulpa.
A non-sentient thoughtform; mental tool or background headspace character.
A headmate based on an external source. Subtypes: fictive (fictional), factive (real).
Introject based on a fictional character. Community portmanteau (Fiction + Active). Retain as loanword — no direct equivalents.
Introject based on a real person. Community portmanteau (Fact + Active) — retain as loanword.
A fictive who identifies deeply with pseudomemories from their source fiction.
Umbrella term for identities partially or wholly non-human.
Headmates identifying as earthly animals.
Headmates identifying as mythical or non-earthly entities.
Community-preferred goal: living harmoniously as a system without merging. Contrast with clinical 'integration'.
Synonym for functional multiplicity.
Consensual merging of two or more headmates. NOT synonymous with singletification (which implies coercion).
Clinical term for fusion. Use carefully — many systems view forced integration as harmful. Prefer 'functional multiplicity' unless describing consensual merging.
Portmanteau of 'system + discourse' — intra-community ideological conflict. Retain as loanword.
Accusing a system of fabricating their experience — a form of harassment. Translate as 'false-accusation' / 'invalidation harassment'.
System medicalist — gatekeeps validity to clinically-diagnosed systems only. Exclusionist; retain as loanword with context note.
One who gatekeeps community membership; synonym for sysmed in this context.
App/brand name.
Dissociative Identity Disorder — keep English medical acronym.
Other Specified Dissociative Disorder — keep English medical acronym.
Third-party app name.
Third-party Discord bot name.
HARMFUL when used to describe headmates. Denies personhood. Prefer 'headmates' / 'system members'. Block MT from generating this in member contexts.
HARMFUL — outdated MPD-era framing. Use 'plurality' / 'multiplicity'.
OBSOLETE — replaced by DID in DSM-IV (1994). Do not use as current terminology; translate as 'DID (formerly MPD)' only in historical context.
CONTESTED clinical-origin term. Accept as synonym but NEVER default primary translation for 'member' / 'headmate'. Many find it dehumanizing.
DEROGATORY pop-culture reference. Do not use; flag if MT generates.
HARMFUL — coerced integration. Reference only when documenting abuse; prefer 'functional multiplicity' as goal language.
